1.name: Account
1.description: Account mnemonic as agreed between buy and sell sides, e.g. broker and institution or investor/intermediary and fund manager.

2.name: AdvId
2.description: Unique identifier of advertisement message. (Prior to FIX 4.1 this field was of type int)

3.name: AdvRefID
3.description: Reference identifier used with CANCEL and REPLACE transaction types. (Prior to FIX 4.1 this field was of type int)

6.name: AvgPx
6.description: Calculated average price of all fills on this order. For Fixed Income trades AvgPx is always expressed as percent-of-par, regardless of the PriceType (423) of LastPx (3). I.e., AvgPx will contain an average of percent-of-par values (see LastParPx (669)) for issues traded in Yield, Spread or Discount.

7.name: BeginSeqNo
7.description: Message sequence number of first message in range to be resent

8.name: BeginString
8.description: Identifies beginning of new message and protocol version. ALWAYS FIRST FIELD IN MESSAGE. (Always unencrypted)

9.name: BodyLength
9.description: Message length, in bytes, forward to the CheckSum field. ALWAYS SECOND FIELD IN MESSAGE. (Always unencrypted)

10.name: CheckSum
10.description: Three byte, simple checksum (see Volume 2: "Checksum Calculation" for description). ALWAYS LAST FIELD IN MESSAGE; i.e. serves, with the trailing <SOH>, as the end-of-message delimiter. Always defined as three characters. (Always unencrypted)

11.name: ClOrdID
11.description: Unique identifier for Order as assigned by the buy-side (institution, broker, intermediary etc.) (identified by SenderCompID (49) or OnBehalfOfCompID (5) as appropriate). Uniqueness must be guaranteed within a single trading day. Firms, particularly those which electronically submit multi-day orders, trade globally or throughout market close periods, should ensure uniqueness across days, for example by embedding a date within the ClOrdID field.

12.name: Commission
12.description: Commission. Note if CommType (3) is percentage, Commission of 5% should be represented as .05.

14.name: CumQty
14.description: Total quantity (e.g. number of shares) filled. (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

15.name: Currency
15.description: Identifies currency used for price. Absence of this field is interpreted as the default for the security. It is recommended that systems provide the currency value whenever possible. See "Appendix 6-A: Valid Currency Codes" for information on obtaining valid values.

16.name: EndSeqNo
16.description: Message sequence number of last message in range to be resent. If request is for a single message BeginSeqNo (7) = EndSeqNo. If request is for all messages subsequent to a particular message, EndSeqNo = "0" (representing infinity).

17.name: ExecID
17.description: Unique identifier of execution message as assigned by sell-side (broker, exchange, ECN) (will be 0 (zero) for ExecType (50) =I (Order Status)). Uniqueness must be guaranteed within a single trading day or the life of a multi-day order. Firms which accept multi-day orders should consider embedding a date within the ExecID field to assure uniqueness across days. (Prior to FIX 4.1 this field was of type int)

18.name: ExecInst
18.description: Instructions for order handling on exchange trading floor. If more than one instruction is applicable to an order, this field can contain multiple instructions separated by space.

19.name: ExecRefID
19.description: Reference identifier used with Trade Cancel and Trade Correct execution types. (Prior to FIX 4.1 this field was of type int)

21.name: HandlInst
21.description: Instructions for order handling on Broker trading floor

22.name: SecurityIDSource
22.description: Identifies class or source of the SecurityID (48) value. Required if SecurityID is specified.

23.name: IOIID
23.description: Unique identifier of IOI message. (Prior to FIX 4.1 this field was of type int)

26.name: IOIRefID
26.description: Reference identifier used with CANCEL and REPLACE, transaction types. (Prior to FIX 4.1 this field was of type int)

27.name: IOIQty
27.description: Quantity (e.g. number of shares) in numeric form or relative size.

30.name: LastMkt
30.description: Market of execution for last fill, or an indication of the market where an order was routed Valid values: See "Appendix 6-C"

32.name: LastQty
32.description: Quantity (e.g. shares) bought/sold on this (last) fill. (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

33.name: NoLinesOfText
33.description: Identifies number of lines of text body

35.name: MsgType
35.description: Defines message type ALWAYS THIRD FIELD IN MESSAGE. (Always unencrypted) Note: A "U" as the first character in the MsgType field (i.e. U, U2, etc) indicates that the message format is privately defined between the sender and receiver.

37.name: OrderID
37.description: Unique identifier for Order as assigned by sell-side (broker, exchange, ECN). Uniqueness must be guaranteed within a single trading day. Firms which accept multi-day orders should consider embedding a date within the OrderID field to assure uniqueness across days.

38.name: OrderQty
38.description: Quantity ordered. This represents the number of shares for equities or par, face or nominal value for FI instruments. (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

39.name: OrdStatus
39.description: Identifies current status of order. *** SOME VALUES HAVE BEEN REPLACED - See "Replaced Features and Supported Approach" *** (see Volume : "Glossary" for value definitions)

40.name: OrdType
40.description: Order type *** SOME VALUES ARE NO LONGER USED - See "Deprecated (Phased-out) Features and Supported Approach" *** (see Volume : "Glossary" for value definitions)

41.name: OrigClOrdID
41.description: ClOrdID () of the previous order (NOT the initial order of the day) as assigned by the institution, used to identify the previous order in cancel and cancel/replace requests.

42.name: OrigTime
42.description: Time of message origination (always expressed in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT"))

43.name: PossDupFlag
43.description: Indicates possible retransmission of message with this sequence number

44.name: Price
44.description: Price per unit of quantity (e.g. per share)

48.name: SecurityID
48.description: Security identifier value of SecurityIDSource (22) type (e.g. CUSIP, SEDOL, ISIN, etc). Requires SecurityIDSource.

49.name: SenderCompID
49.description: Assigned value used to identify firm sending message.

50.name: SenderSubID
50.description: Assigned value used to identify specific message originator (desk, trader, etc.)

52.name: SendingTime
52.description: Time of message transmission (always expressed in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT")

53.name: Quantity
53.description: Overall/total quantity (e.g. number of shares) (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

55.name: Symbol
55.description: Ticker symbol. Common, "human understood" representation of the security. SecurityID (48) value can be specified if no symbol exists (e.g. non-exchange traded Collective Investment Vehicles) Use "[N/A]" for products which do not have a symbol.

56.name: TargetCompID
56.description: Assigned value used to identify receiving firm.

57.name: TargetSubID
57.description: Assigned value used to identify specific individual or unit intended to receive message. "ADMIN" reserved for administrative messages not intended for a specific user.

58.name: Text
58.description: Free format text string (Note: this field does not have a specified maximum length)

59.name: TimeInForce
59.description: Specifies how long the order remains in effect. Absence of this field is interpreted as DAY.

60.name: TransactTime
60.description: Time of execution/order creation (expressed in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT")

62.name: ValidUntilTime
62.description: Indicates expiration time of indication message (always expressed in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT")

63.name: SettlType
63.description: Indicates order settlement period. If present, SettlDate (64) overrides this field. If both SettlType (63) and SettDate (64) are omitted, the default for SettlType (63) is 0 (Regular) Regular is defined as the default settlement period for the particular security on the exchange of execution. In Fixed Income the contents of this field may influence the instrument definition if the SecurityID (48) is ambiguous. In the US an active Treasury offering may be re-opened, and for a time one CUSIP will apply to both the current and "when-issued" securities. Supplying a value of "7" clarifies the instrument description; any other value or the absence of this field should cause the respondent to default to the active issue.

64.name: SettlDate
64.description: Specific date of trade settlement (SettlementDate) in YYYYMMDD format. If present, this field overrides SettlType (63). This field is required if the value of SettlType (63) is 6 (Future) or 8 (Sellers Option). This field must be omitted if the value of SettlType (63) is 7 (When and If Issued) (expressed in local time at place of settlement)

65.name: SymbolSfx
65.description: Additional information about the security (e.g. preferred, warrants, etc.). Note also see SecurityType (67). Valid values: As defined in the NYSE Stock and bond Symbol Directory and in the AMEX Fitch Directory Fixed Income use: WI = "When Issued" for a security to be reissued under an old CUSIP or ISIN CD = a EUCP with lump-sum interest rather than discount price

66.name: ListID
66.description: Unique identifier for list as assigned by institution, used to associate multiple individual orders. Uniqueness must be guaranteed within a single trading day. Firms which generate multi-day orders should consider embedding a date within the ListID field to assure uniqueness across days.

67.name: ListSeqNo
67.description: Sequence of individual order within list (i.e. ListSeqNo of TotNoOrders (68), 2 of 25, 3 of 25, . . . )

68.name: TotNoOrders
68.description: Total number of list order entries across all messages. Should be the sum of all NoOrders (73) in each message that has repeating list order entries related to the same ListID (66). Used to support fragmentation. (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was named "ListNoOrds")

69.name: ListExecInst
69.description: Free format text message containing list handling and execution instructions.

70.name: AllocID
70.description: Unique identifier for allocation message. (Prior to FIX 4.1 this field was of type int)

71.name: AllocTransType
71.description: Identifies allocation transaction type *** SOME VALUES HAVE BEEN REPLACED - See "Replaced Features and Supported Approach" ***

72.name: RefAllocID
72.description: Reference identifier to be used with AllocTransType (7) =Replace or Cancel. (Prior to FIX 4.1 this field was of type int)

73.name: NoOrders
73.description: Indicates number of orders to be combined for average pricing and allocation.

74.name: AvgPxPrecision
74.description: Indicates number of decimal places to be used for average pricing. Absence of this field indicates that default precision arranged by the broker/institution is to be used.

75.name: TradeDate
75.description: Indicates date of trade referenced in this message in YYYYMMDD format. Absence of this field indicates current day (expressed in local time at place of trade).

77.name: PositionEffect
77.description: Indicates whether the resulting position after a trade should be an opening position or closing position. Used for omnibus accounting - where accounts are held on a gross basis instead of being netted together.

80.name: AllocQty
80.description: Quantity to be allocated to specific sub-account (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

81.name: ProcessCode
81.description: Processing code for sub-account. Absence of this field in AllocAccount (79) / AllocPrice (366) /AllocQty (80) / ProcessCode instance indicates regular trade.

82.name: NoRpts
82.description: Total number of reports within series.

83.name: RptSeq
83.description: Sequence number of message within report series.

84.name: CxlQty
84.description: Total quantity canceled for this order. (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

85.name: NoDlvyInst
85.description: Number of delivery instruction fields in repeating group. Note this field was removed in FIX 4.1 and reinstated in FIX 4.4.

93.name: SignatureLength
93.description: Number of bytes in signature field.

95.name: RawDataLength
95.description: Number of bytes in raw data field.

96.name: RawData
96.description: Unformatted raw data, can include bitmaps, word processor documents, etc.

97.name: PossResend
97.description: Indicates that message may contain information that has been sent under another sequence number.

99.name: StopPx
99.description: Price per unit of quantity (e.g. per share)

100.name: ExDestination
100.description: Execution destination as defined by institution when order is entered. Valid values: See "Appendix 6-C"

102.name: CxlRejReason
102.description: Code to identify reason for cancel rejection

103.name: OrdRejReason
103.description: Code to identify reason for order rejection.

106.name: Issuer
106.description: Name of security issuer (e.g. International Business Machines, GNMA). see also Volume 7: "PRODUCT: FIXED INCOME - Euro Issuer Values"

110.name: MinQty
110.description: Minimum quantity of an order to be executed. (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

111.name: MaxFloor
111.description: Maximum quantity (e.g. number of shares) within an order to be shown on the exchange floor at any given time. (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

112.name: TestReqID
112.description: Identifier included in Test Request message to be returned in resulting Heartbeat

113.name: ReportToExch
113.description: Identifies party of trade responsible for exchange reporting.

114.name: LocateReqd
114.description: Indicates whether the broker is to locate the stock in conjunction with a short sell order.

115.name: OnBehalfOfCompID
115.description: Assigned value used to identify firm originating message if the message was delivered by a third party i.e. the third party firm identifier would be delivered in the SenderCompID field and the firm originating the message in this field.

116.name: OnBehalfOfSubID
116.description: Assigned value used to identify specific message originator (i.e. trader) if the message was delivered by a third party

118.name: NetMoney
118.description: Total amount due as the result of the transaction (e.g. for Buy order - principal + commission + fees) reported in currency of execution.

119.name: SettlCurrAmt
119.description: Total amount due expressed in settlement currency (includes the effect of the forex transaction)

121.name: ForexReq
121.description: Indicates request for forex accommodation trade to be executed along with security transaction.

122.name: OrigSendingTime
122.description: Original time of message transmission (always expressed in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT") when transmitting orders as the result of a resend request.

123.name: GapFillFlag
123.description: Indicates that the Sequence Reset message is replacing administrative or application messages which will not be resent.

124.name: NoExecs
124.description: No of execution repeating group entries to follow.

126.name: ExpireTime
126.description: Time/Date of order expiration (always expressed in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT") The meaning of expiration is specific to the context where the field is used. For orders, this is the expiration time of a Good Til Date TimeInForce. For Quotes - this is the expiration of the quote. Expiration time is provided across the quote message dialog to control the length of time of the overall quoting process. For collateral requests, this is the time by which collateral must be assigned. For collateral assignments, this is the time by which a response to the assignment is expected.

128.name: DeliverToCompID
128.description: Assigned value used to identify the firm targeted to receive the message if the message is delivered by a third party i.e. the third party firm identifier would be delivered in the TargetCompID (56) field and the ultimate receiver firm ID in this field.

129.name: DeliverToSubID
129.description: Assigned value used to identify specific message recipient (i.e. trader) if the message is delivered by a third party

130.name: IOINaturalFlag
130.description: Indicates that IOI is the result of an existing agency order or a facilitation position resulting from an agency order, not from principal trading or order solicitation activity.

134.name: BidSize
134.description: Quantity of bid (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

135.name: OfferSize
135.description: Quantity of offer (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

136.name: NoMiscFees
136.description: Number of repeating groups of miscellaneous fees

141.name: ResetSeqNumFlag
141.description: Indicates that the both sides of the FIX session should reset sequence numbers.

142.name: SenderLocationID
142.description: Assigned value used to identify specific message originator’s location (i.e. geographic location and/or desk, trader)

143.name: TargetLocationID
143.description: Assigned value used to identify specific message destination’s location (i.e. geographic location and/or desk, trader)

144.name: OnBehalfOfLocationID
144.description: Assigned value used to identify specific message originator’s location (i.e. geographic location and/or desk, trader) if the message was delivered by a third party

145.name: DeliverToLocationID
145.description: Assigned value used to identify specific message recipient’s location (i.e. geographic location and/or desk, trader) if the message was delivered by a third party

146.name: NoRelatedSym
146.description: Specifies the number of repeating symbols specified.

147.name: Subject
147.description: The subject of an Email message

148.name: Headline
148.description: The headline of a News message

149.name: URLLink
149.description: A URI (Uniform Resource Identifier) or URL (Uniform Resource Locator) link to additional information (i.e. http://www.XYZ.com/research.html) See "Appendix 6-B FIX Fields Based Upon Other Standards"

150.name: ExecType
150.description: Describes the specific ExecutionRpt (i.e. Pending Cancel) while OrdStatus (39) will always identify the current order status (i.e. Partially Filled) *** SOME VALUES HAVE BEEN REPLACED - See "Replaced Features and Supported Approach" ***

151.name: LeavesQty
151.description: Quantity open for further execution. If the OrdStatus (39) is Canceled, DoneForTheDay, Expired, Calculated, or Rejected (in which case the order is no longer active) then LeavesQty could be 0, otherwise LeavesQty = OrderQty (38) – CumQty (4). (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

152.name: CashOrderQty
152.description: Specifies the approximate order quantity desired in total monetary units vs. as tradeable units (e.g. number of shares). The broker or fund manager (for CIV orders) would be responsible for converting and calculating a tradeable unit (e.g. share) quantity (OrderQty (38)) based upon this amount to be used for the actual order and subsequent messages.

153.name: AllocAvgPx
153.description: AvgPx (6) for a specific AllocAccount (79) For Fixed Income this is always expressed as "percent of par" price type.

155.name: SettlCurrFxRate
155.description: Foreign exchange rate used to compute SettlCurrAmt (9) from Currency (5) to SettlCurrency (20)

156.name: SettlCurrFxRateCalc
156.description: Specifies whether or not SettlCurrFxRate (55) should be multiplied or divided

157.name: NumDaysInterest
157.description: Number of Days of Interest for convertible bonds and fixed income. Note value may be negative.

158.name: AccruedInterestRate
158.description: The amount the buyer compensates the seller for the portion of the next coupon interest payment the seller has earned but will not receive from the issuer because the issuer will send the next coupon payment to the buyer. Accrued Interest Rate is the annualized Accrued Interest amount divided by the purchase price of the bond.

159.name: AccruedInterestAmt
159.description: Amount of Accrued Interest for convertible bonds and fixed income

160.name: SettlInstMode
160.description: Indicates mode used for Settlement Instructions message. *** SOME VALUES HAVE BEEN REPLACED - See "Replaced Features and Supported Approach" ***

161.name: AllocText
161.description: Free format text related to a specific AllocAccount (79).

164.name: EmailThreadID
164.description: Unique identifier for an email thread (new and chain of replies)

167.name: SecurityType
167.description: Indicates type of security. See also the Product (460) and CFICode (46) fields. It is recommended that CFICode be used instead of SecurityType for non-Fixed Income instruments. Example values (grouped by Product field value) (Note: additional values may be used by mutual agreement of the counterparties): * Identify the Issuer in the "Issuer" field(06) *** REPLACED values - See "Replaced Features and Supported Approach" *** NOTE: Additional values may be used by mutual agreement of the counterparties)

168.name: EffectiveTime
168.description: Time the details within the message should take effect (always expressed in UTC (Universal Time Coordinated, also known as "GMT")

169.name: StandInstDbType
169.description: Identifies the Standing Instruction database used

170.name: StandInstDbName
170.description: Name of the Standing Instruction database represented with StandInstDbType (69) (i.e. the Global Custodian’s name).

171.name: StandInstDbID
171.description: Unique identifier used on the Standing Instructions database for the Standing Instructions to be referenced.

189.name: BidForwardPoints
189.description: Bid F/X forward points added to spot rate. May be a negative value.

191.name: OfferForwardPoints
191.description: Offer F/X forward points added to spot rate. May be a negative value.

192.name: OrderQty2
192.description: OrderQty (38) of the future part of a F/X swap order.

193.name: SettlDate2
193.description: SettDate (64) of the future part of a F/X swap order.

195.name: LastForwardPoints
195.description: F/X forward points added to LastSpotRate (94). May be a negative value.

196.name: AllocLinkID
196.description: Can be used to link two different Allocation messages (each with unique AllocID (70)) together, i.e. for F/X "Netting" or "Swaps". Should be unique.

197.name: AllocLinkType
197.description: Identifies the type of Allocation linkage when AllocLinkID (96) is used.

198.name: SecondaryOrderID
198.description: Assigned by the party which accepts the order. Can be used to provide the OrderID (37) used by an exchange or executing system.

199.name: NoIOIQualifiers
199.description: Number of repeating groups of IOIQualifiers (04).

200.name: MaturityMonthYear
200.description: Can be used with standardized derivatives vs. the MaturityDate (54) field. Month and Year of the maturity (used for standardized futures and options). Format: YYYYMM (i.e. 199903) YYYYMMDD (20030323) YYYYMMwN (200303w) for week A specific date or can be appended to the MaturityMonthYear. For instance, if multiple standard products exist that mature in the same Year and Month, but actually mature at a different time, a value can be appended, such as "w" or "w2" to indicate week as opposed to week 2 expiration. Likewise, the date (0-3) can be appended to indicate a specific expiration (maturity date).

201.name: PutOrCall
201.description: Indicates whether an Option is for a put or call

203.name: CoveredOrUncovered
203.description: Used for derivative products, such as options

206.name: OptAttribute
206.description: Can be used for SecurityType (67) =OPT to identify a particular security. Valid values vary by SecurityExchange: *** REPLACED values - See "Replaced Features and Supported Approach" *** For Exchange: MONEP (Paris) L = Long (a.k.a. "American") S = Short (a.k.a. "European") For Exchanges: DTB (Frankfurt), HKSE (Hong Kong), and SOFFEX (Zurich) 0-9 = single digit "version" number assigned by exchange following capital adjustments (0=current, =prior, 2=prior to , etc).

207.name: SecurityExchange
207.description: Market used to help identify a security. Valid values: See "Appendix 6-C"

208.name: NotifyBrokerOfCredit
208.description: Indicates whether or not details should be communicated to BrokerOfCredit (i.e. step-in broker).

209.name: AllocHandlInst
209.description: Indicates how the receiver (i.e. third party) of Allocation message should handle/process the account details

210.name: MaxShow
210.description: Maximum quantity (e.g. number of shares) within an order to be shown to other customers (i.e. sent via an IOI). (Prior to FIX 4.2 this field was of type int)

211.name: PegOffsetValue
211.description: Amount (signed) added to the peg for a pegged order in the context of the PegOffsetType (836) (Prior to FIX 4.4 this field was of type PriceOffset)

212.name: XmlDataLen
212.description: Length of the XmlData data block.

213.name: XmlData
213.description: Actual XML data stream (e.g. FIXML). See approriate XML reference (e.g. FIXML). Note: may contain embedded SOH characters.

214.name: SettlInstRefID
214.description: Reference identifier for the SettlInstID (62) with Cancel and Replace SettlInstTransType (63) transaction types.

215.name: NoRoutingIDs
215.description: Number of repeating groups of RoutingID (27) and RoutingType (26) values. See Volume 3: "Pre-Trade Message Targeting/Routing"

216.name: RoutingType
216.description: Indicates the type of RoutingID (27) specified

217.name: RoutingID
217.description: Assigned value used to identify a specific routing destination.

218.name: Spread
218.description: For Fixed Income. Either Swap Spread or Spread to Benchmark depending upon the order type. Spread to Benchmark: Basis points relative to a benchmark. To be expressed as "count of basis points" (vs. an absolute value). E.g. High Grade Corporate Bonds may express price as basis points relative to benchmark (the BenchmarkCurveName (22) field). Note: Basis points can be negative. Swap Spread: Target spread for a swap.

220.name: BenchmarkCurveCurrency
220.description: Identifies currency used for benchmark curve. See "Appendix 6-A: Valid Currency Codes" for information on obtaining valid values. (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3)

222.name: BenchmarkCurvePoint
222.description: Point on benchmark curve. Free form values: e.g. "Y", "7Y", "INTERPOLATED". Sample values: M = combination of a number between 1-12 and a "M" for month Y = combination of number between 1-100 and a "Y" for year} 10Y-OLD = see above, then add "-OLD" when appropriate INTERPOLATED = the point is mathematically derived 2/2031 5 3/8 = the point is stated via a combination of maturity month / year and coupon See Fixed Income-specific documentation at http://www.fixprotocol.org for additional values. (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3)

223.name: CouponRate
223.description: The rate of interest that, when multiplied by the principal, par value, or face value of a bond, provides the currency amount of the periodic interest payment. The coupon is always cited, along with maturity, in any quotation of a bond's price.

224.name: CouponPaymentDate
224.description: Date interest is to be paid. Used in identifying Corporate Bond issues. (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3) (prior to FIX 4.4 field was of type UTCDate)

225.name: IssueDate
225.description: The date on which a bond or stock offering is issued. It may or may not be the same as the effective date ("Dated Date") or the date on which interest begins to accrue ("Interest Accrual Date") (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3) (prior to FIX 4.4 field was of type UTCDate)

226.name: RepurchaseTerm
226.description: *** DEPRECATED FIELD - See "Deprecated (Phased-out) Features and Supported Approach" *** Number of business days before repurchase of a repo. (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3)

227.name: RepurchaseRate
227.description: *** DEPRECATED FIELD - See "Deprecated (Phased-out) Features and Supported Approach" *** Percent of par at which a Repo will be repaid. Represented as a percent, e.g. .9525 represents 95-/4 percent of par. (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3)

228.name: Factor
228.description: For Fixed Income: Amorization Factor for deriving Current face from Original face for ABS or MBS securities, note the fraction may be greater than, equal to or less than . In TIPS securities this is the Inflation index. Qty * Factor * Price = Gross Trade Amount For Derivatives: Contract Value Factor by which price must be adjusted to determine the true nominal value of one futures/options contract. (Qty * Price) * Factor = Nominal Value (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3)

229.name: TradeOriginationDate
229.description: Used with Fixed Income for Muncipal New Issue Market. Agreement in principal between counter-parties prior to actual trade date. (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3) (prior to FIX 4.4 field was of type UTCDate)

230.name: ExDate
230.description: The date when a distribution of interest is deducted from a securities assets or set aside for payment to bondholders. On the ex-date, the securities price drops by the amount of the distribution (plus or minus any market activity). (Note tag # was reserved in FIX 4.1, added in FIX 4.3) (prior to FIX 4.4 field was of type UTCDate)

231.name: ContractMultiplier
231.description: Specifies the ratio or multiply factor to convert from "nominal" units (e.g. contracts) to total units (e.g. shares) (e.g. 1.0, 100, 1000, etc). Applicable For Fixed Income, Convertible Bonds, Derivatives, etc. In general quantities for all calsses should be expressed in the basic unit of the instrument, e.g. shares for equities, norminal or par amount for bonds, currency for foreign exchange. When quantity is expressed in contracts, e.g. financing transactions and bond trade reporting, ContractMutliplier should contain the number of units in one contract and can be omitted if the multiplier is the default amount for the instrument, i.e. 1,000 par of bonds, 1,000,000 par for financing transactions.

459.description: Identifies class or source of the UnderlyingSecurityAltID (458) value. Required if UnderlyingSecurityAltID is specified. Valid values: Same valid values as the SecurityIDSource (22) field

460.name: Product
460.description: Indicates the type of product the security is associated with. See also the CFICode (46) and SecurityType (67) fields.

461.name: CFICode
461.description: Indicates the type of security using ISO 0962 standard, Classification of Financial Instruments (CFI code) values. ISO 0962 is maintained by ANNA (Association of National Numbering Agencies) acting as Registration Authority. See "Appendix 6-B FIX Fields Based Upon Other Standards". See also the Product (460) and SecurityType (67) fields. It is recommended that CFICode be used instead of SecurityType (67) for non-Fixed Income instruments. A subset of possible values applicable to FIX usage are identified in "Appendix 6-D CFICode Usage - ISO 0962 Classification of Financial Instruments (CFI code)"

462.name: UnderlyingProduct
462.description: Underlying security’s Product. Valid values: see Product(460) field

463.name: UnderlyingCFICode
463.description: Underlying security’s CFICode. Valid values: see CFICode (46)field

464.name: TestMessageIndicator
464.description: Indicates whether or not this FIX Session is a "test" vs. "production" connection. Useful for preventing "accidents".

466.name: BookingRefID
466.description: Common reference passed to a post-trade booking process (e.g. industry matching utility).

467.name: IndividualAllocID
467.description: Unique identifier for a specific NoAllocs (78) repeating group instance (e.g. for an AllocAccount).

468.name: RoundingDirection
468.description: Specifies which direction to round For CIV – indicates whether or not the quantity of shares/units is to be rounded and in which direction where CashOrdQty (52) or (for CIV only) OrderPercent (56) are specified on an order. The default is for rounding to be at the discretion of the executing broker or fund manager. e.g. for an order specifying CashOrdQty or OrderPercent if the calculated number of shares/units was 325.76 and RoundingModulus (469) was 0 – "round down" would give 320 units, "round up" would give 330 units and "round to nearest" would give 320 units.

469.name: RoundingModulus
469.description: For CIV - a float value indicating the value to which rounding is required. i.e. 0 means round to a multiple of 0 units/shares; 0.5 means round to a multiple of 0.5 units/shares. The default, if RoundingDirection (468) is specified without RoundingModulus, is to round to a whole unit/share.

470.name: CountryOfIssue
470.description: ISO Country code of instrument issue (e.g. the country portion typically used in ISIN). Can be used in conjunction with non-ISIN SecurityID (48) (e.g. CUSIP for Municipal Bonds without ISIN) to provide uniqueness.

471.name: StateOrProvinceOfIssue
471.description: A two-character state or province abbreviation.

472.name: LocaleOfIssue
472.description: Identifies the locale. For Municipal Security Issuers other than state or province. Refer to http://www.atmos.albany.edu/cgi/stagrep-cgi Reference the IATA city codes for values. Note IATA (International Air Transport Association) maintains the codes at www.iata.org.

473.name: NoRegistDtls
473.description: The number of registration details on a Registration Instructions message

474.name: MailingDtls
474.description: Set of Correspondence address details, possibly including phone, fax, etc.

475.name: InvestorCountryOfResidence
475.description: The ISO 366 Country code (2 character) identifying which country the beneficial investor is resident for tax purposes.

476.name: PaymentRef
476.description: "Settlement Payment Reference" – A free format Payment reference to assist with reconciliation, e.g. a Client and/or Order ID number.

477.name: DistribPaymentMethod
477.description: A code identifying the payment method for a (fractional) distribution. 13 through 998 are reserved for future use Values above 000 are available for use by private agreement among counterparties

478.name: CashDistribCurr
478.description: Specifies currency to be use for Cash Distributions– see "Appendix 6-A; Valid Currency Codes".

479.name: CommCurrency
479.description: Specifies currency to be use for Commission (2) if the Commission currency is different from the Deal Currency - see "Appendix 6-A; Valid Currency Codes".

480.name: CancellationRights
480.description: For CIV – A one character code identifying whether Cancellation rights/Cooling off period applies

481.name: MoneyLaunderingStatus
481.description: A one character code identifying Money laundering status

482.name: MailingInst
482.description: Free format text to specify mailing instruction requirements, e.g. "no third party mailings".

483.name: TransBkdTime
483.description: For CIV A date and time stamp to indicate the time a CIV order was booked by the fund manager.

484.name: ExecPriceType
484.description: For CIV - Identifies how the execution price LastPx (3) was calculated from the fund unit/share price(s) calculated at the fund valuation point

485.name: ExecPriceAdjustment
485.description: For CIV the amount or percentage by which the fund unit/share price was adjusted, as indicated by ExecPriceType (484)

486.name: DateOfBirth
486.description: The date of birth applicable to the individual, e.g. required to open some types of tax-exempt account.

487.name: TradeReportTransType
487.description: Identifies Trade Report message transaction type (Prior to FIX 4.4 this field was of type char)

488.name: CardHolderName
488.description: The name of the payment card holder as specified on the card being used for payment.

489.name: CardNumber
489.description: The number of the payment card as specified on the card being used for payment.

490.name: CardExpDate
490.description: The expiry date of the payment card as specified on the card being used for payment.

491.name: CardIssNum
491.description: The issue number of the payment card as specified on the card being used for payment. This is only applicable to certain types of card.

492.name: PaymentMethod
492.description: A code identifying the Settlement payment method. 16 through 998 are reserved for future use Values above 000 are available for use by private agreement among counterparties

493.name: RegistAcctType
493.description: For CIV – a fund manager-defined code identifying which of the fund manager’s account types is required.

494.name: Designation
494.description: Free format text defining the designation to be associated with a holding on the register. Used to identify assets of a specific underlying investor using a common registration, e.g. a broker’s nominee or street name.

495.name: TaxAdvantageType
495.description: For CIV - a code identifying the type of tax exempt account in which purchased shares/units are to be held

496.name: RegistRejReasonText
496.description: Text indicating reason(s) why a Registration Instruction has been rejected.

497.name: FundRenewWaiv
497.description: A one character code identifying whether the Fund based renewal commission is to be waived.

498.name: CashDistribAgentName
498.description: Name of local agent bank if for cash distributions

499.name: CashDistribAgentCode
499.description: BIC (Bank Identification Code--Swift managed) code of agent bank for cash distributions

500.name: CashDistribAgentAcctNumber
500.description: Account number at agent bank for distributions.

501.name: CashDistribPayRef
501.description: Free format Payment reference to assist with reconciliation of distributions.

502.name: CashDistribAgentAcctName
502.description: Name of account at agent bank for distributions.

503.name: CardStartDate
503.description: The start date of the card as specified on the card being used for payment.

504.name: PaymentDate
504.description: The date written on a cheque or date payment should be submitted to the relevant clearing system.

505.name: PaymentRemitterID
505.description: Identifies sender of a payment, e.g. the payment remitter or a customer reference number.

506.name: RegistStatus
506.description: Registration status as returned by the broker or (for CIV) the fund manager

507.name: RegistRejReasonCode
507.description: Reason(s) why Registration Instructions has been rejected. The reason may be further amplified in the RegistRejReasonCode field. Possible values of reason code include:

508.name: RegistRefID
508.description: Reference identifier for the RegistID (53) with Cancel and Replace RegistTransType (54) transaction types.

509.name: RegistDtls
509.description: Set of Registration name and address details, possibly including phone, fax etc.

510.name: NoDistribInsts
510.description: The number of Distribution Instructions on a Registration Instructions message

511.name: RegistEmail
511.description: Email address relating to Registration name and address details

512.name: DistribPercentage
512.description: The amount of each distribution to go to this beneficiary, expressed as a percentage

513.name: RegistID
513.description: Unique identifier of the registration details as assigned by institution or intermediary.

515.name: ExecValuationPoint
515.description: For CIV - a date and time stamp to indicate the fund valuation point with respect to which a order was priced by the fund manager.

516.name: OrderPercent
516.description: For CIV specifies the approximate order quantity desired. For a CIV Sale it specifies percentage of investor’s total holding to be sold. For a CIV switch/exchange it specifies percentage of investor’s cash realised from sales to be re-invested. The executing broker, intermediary or fund manager is responsible for converting and calculating OrderQty (38) in shares/units for subsequent messages.

518.name: NoContAmts
518.description: The number of Contract Amount details on an Execution Report message

519.name: ContAmtType
519.description: Type of ContAmtValue (520). NOTE That Commission Amount / % in Contract Amounts is the commission actually charged, rather than the commission instructions given in Fields 2/3.

520.name: ContAmtValue
520.description: Value of Contract Amount, e.g. a financial amount or percentage as indicated by ContAmtType (59).

521.name: ContAmtCurr
521.description: Specifies currency for the Contract amount if different from the Deal Currency - see "Appendix 6-A; Valid Currency Codes".

523.name: PartySubID
523.description: Sub-identifier (e.g. Clearing Account for PartyRole (452)=Clearing Firm, Locate ID # for PartyRole=Locate/Lending Firm, etc). Not required when using PartyID (448), PartyIDSource (447), and PartyRole.

524.name: NestedPartyID
524.description: PartyID value within a nested repeating group. Same values as PartyID (448)

525.name: NestedPartyIDSource
525.description: PartyIDSource value within a nested repeating group. Same values as PartyIDSource (447)

526.name: SecondaryClOrdID
526.description: Assigned by the party which originates the order. Can be used to provide the ClOrdID (11) used by an exchange or executing system.

527.name: SecondaryExecID
527.description: Assigned by the party which accepts the order. Can be used to provide the ExecID (7) used by an exchange or executing system.

528.name: OrderCapacity
528.description: Designates the capacity of the firm placing the order (as of FIX 4.3, this field replaced Rule80A (tag 47) --used in conjunction with OrderRestrictions (529) field) (see Volume : "Glossary" for value definitions)

529.name: OrderRestrictions
529.description: Restrictions associated with an order. If more than one restriction is applicable to an order, this field can contain multiple instructions separated by space.

530.name: MassCancelRequestType
530.description: Specifies scope of Order Mass Cancel Request

531.name: MassCancelResponse
531.description: Specifies the action taken by counterparty order handling system as a result of the Order Mass Cancel Request

532.name: MassCancelRejectReason
532.description: Reason Order Mass Cancel Request was rejected

533.name: TotalAffectedOrders
533.description: Total number of orders affected by mass cancel request.

534.name: NoAffectedOrders
534.description: Number of affected orders in the repeating group of order ids.

535.name: AffectedOrderID
535.description: OrderID (37) of an order affected by a mass cancel request.

536.name: AffectedSecondaryOrderID
536.description: SecondaryOrderID (98) of an order affected by a mass cancel request.

537.name: QuoteType
537.description: Identifies the type of quote. An indicative quote is used to inform a counterparty of a market. An indicative quote does not result directly in a trade. A tradeable quote is submitted to a market and will result directly in a trade against other orders and quotes in a market. A restricted tradeable quote is submitted to a market and within a certain restriction (possibly based upon price or quantity) will automatically trade against orders. Order that do not comply with restrictions are sent to the quote issuer who can choose to accept or decline the order. A counter quote is used in the negotiation model. See Volume 7 – Product: Fixed Income for example usage.

538.name: NestedPartyRole
538.description: PartyRole value within a nested repeating group. Same values as PartyRole (452)

539.name: NoNestedPartyIDs
539.description: Number of NestedPartyID (524), NestedPartyIDSource (525), and NestedPartyRole (538) entries

540.name: TotalAccruedInterestAmt
540.description: *** DEPRECATED FIELD - See "Deprecated (Phased-out) Features and Supported Approach" *** Total Amount of Accrued Interest for convertible bonds and fixed income

542.name: UnderlyingMaturityDate
542.description: Underlying security’s maturity date. See MaturityDate (54) field for description

543.name: InstrRegistry
543.description: The location at which records of ownership are maintained for this instrument, and at which ownership changes must be recorded.

544.name: CashMargin
544.description: Identifies whether an order is a margin order or a non-margin order. This is primarily used when sending orders to Japanese exchanges to indicate sell margin or buy to cover. The same tag could be assigned also by buy-side to indicate the intent to sell or buy margin and the sell-side to accept or reject (base on some validation criteria) the margin request.

545.name: NestedPartySubID
545.description: PartySubID value within a nested repeating group. Same values as PartySubID (523)

546.name: Scope
546.description: Defines the scope of a data element

547.name: MDImplicitDelete
547.description: Defines how a server handles distribution of a truncated book. Defaults to broker option.

548.name: CrossID
548.description: Identifier for a cross order. Must be unique during a given trading day. Recommend that firms use the order date as part of the CrossID for Good Till Cancel (GT) orders.

549.name: CrossType
549.description: Type of cross being submitted to a market

550.name: CrossPrioritization
550.description: Indicates if one side or the other of a cross order should be prioritized. The definition of prioritization is left to the market. In some markets prioritization means which side of the cross order is applied to the market first. In other markets – prioritization may mean that the prioritized side is fully executed (sometimes referred to as the side being protected).

551.name: OrigCrossID
551.description: CrossID of the previous cross order (NOT the initial cross order of the day) as assigned by the institution, used to identify the previous cross order in Cross Cancel and Cross Cancel/Replace Requests.

552.name: NoSides
552.description: Number of Side repeating group instances.

555.name: NoLegs
555.description: Number of InstrumentLeg repeating group instances.

556.name: LegCurrency
556.description: Currency associated with a particular Leg's quantity

557.name: TotNoSecurityTypes
557.description: Indicates total number of security types in the event that multiple Security Type messages are used to return results (Prior to FIX 4.4 this field was named TotalNumSecurityTypes)

558.name: NoSecurityTypes
558.description: Number of Security Type repeating group instances.

559.name: SecurityListRequestType
559.description: Identifies the type/criteria of Security List Request

560.name: SecurityRequestResult
560.description: The results returned to a Security Request message

561.name: RoundLot
561.description: The trading lot size of a security

562.name: MinTradeVol
562.description: The minimum trading volume for a security

563.name: MultiLegRptTypeReq
563.description: Indicates the method of execution reporting requested by issuer of the order

564.name: LegPositionEffect
564.description: PositionEffect for leg of a multileg See PositionEffect (77) field for description

565.name: LegCoveredOrUncovered
565.description: CoveredOrUncovered for leg of a multileg See CoveredOrUncovered (203) field for description

566.name: LegPrice
566.description: Price for leg of a multileg See Price (44) field for description

567.name: TradSesStatusRejReason
567.description: Indicates the reason a Trading Session Status Request was rejected

570.name: PreviouslyReported
570.description: Indicates if the trade capture report was previously reported to the counterparty

571.name: TradeReportID
571.description: Unique identifier of trade capture report

572.name: TradeReportRefID
572.description: Reference identifier used with CANCEL and REPLACE transaction types.

573.name: MatchStatus
573.description: The status of this trade with respect to matching or comparison

574.name: MatchType
574.description: The point in the matching process at which this trade was matched

575.name: OddLot
575.description: This trade is to be treated as an odd lot

577.name: ClearingInstruction
577.description: Eligibility of this trade for clearing and central counterparty processing

578.name: TradeInputSource
578.description: Type of input device or system from which the trade was entered.

579.name: TradeInputDevice
579.description: Specific device number, terminal number or station where trade was entered

580.name: NoDates
580.description: Number of Date fields provided in date range

581.name: AccountType
581.description: Type of account associated with an order

582.name: CustOrderCapacity
582.description: Capacity of customer placing the order Primarily used by futures exchanges to indicate the CTICode (customer type indicator) as required by the US CFTC (Commodity Futures Trading Commission).

583.name: ClOrdLinkID
583.description: Permits order originators to tie together groups of orders in which trades resulting from orders are associated for a specific purpose, for example the calculation of average execution price for a customer or to associate lists submitted to a broker as waves of a larger program trade.

584.name: MassStatusReqID
584.description: Value assigned by issuer of Mass Status Request to uniquely identify the request

586.name: OrigOrdModTime
586.description: The most recent (or current) modification TransactTime (tag 60) reported on an Execution Report for the order. The OrigOrdModTime is provided as an optional field on Order Cancel Request and Order Cancel Replace Requests to identify that the state of the order has not changed since the request was issued. This is provided to support markets similar to Eurex and A/C/E.

589.name: DayBookingInst
589.description: Indicates whether or not automatic booking can occur.

590.name: BookingUnit
590.description: Indicates what constitutes a bookable unit.

592.name: UnderlyingCountryOfIssue
592.description: Underlying security’s CountryOfIssue. See CountryOfIssue (470) field for description

593.name: UnderlyingStateOrProvinceOfIssue
593.description: Underlying security’s StateOrProvinceOfIssue. See StateOrProvinceOfIssue (471) field for description

594.name: UnderlyingLocaleOfIssue
594.description: Underlying security’s LocaleOfIssue. See LocaleOfIssue (472) field for description

595.name: UnderlyingInstrRegistry
595.description: Underlying security’s InstrRegistry. See InstrRegistry (543) field for description

596.name: LegCountryOfIssue
596.description: Multileg instrument's individual leg security’s CountryOfIssue. See CountryOfIssue (470) field for description

597.name: LegStateOrProvinceOfIssue
597.description: Multileg instrument's individual leg security’s StateOrProvinceOfIssue. See StateOrProvinceOfIssue (471) field for description

598.name: LegLocaleOfIssue
598.description: Multileg instrument's individual leg security’s LocaleOfIssue. See LocaleOfIssue (472) field for description

599.name: LegInstrRegistry
599.description: Multileg instrument's individual leg security’s InstrRegistry. See InstrRegistry (543) field for description

600.name: LegSymbol
600.description: Multileg instrument's individual security’s Symbol. See Symbol (55) field for description

601.name: LegSymbolSfx
601.description: Multileg instrument's individual security’s SymbolSfx. See SymbolSfx (65) field for description

602.name: LegSecurityID
602.description: Multileg instrument's individual security’s SecurityID. See SecurityID (48) field for description

603.name: LegSecurityIDSource
603.description: Multileg instrument's individual security’s SecurityIDSource. See SecurityIDSource (22) field for description

604.name: NoLegSecurityAltID
604.description: Multileg instrument's individual security’s NoSecurityAltID. See NoSecurityAltID (454) field for description

605.name: LegSecurityAltID
605.description: Multileg instrument's individual security’s SecurityAltID. See SecurityAltID (455) field for description

606.name: LegSecurityAltIDSource
606.description: Multileg instrument's individual security’s SecurityAltIDSource. See SecurityAltIDSource (456) field for description

607.name: LegProduct
607.description: Multileg instrument's individual security’s Product. See Product (460) field for description

608.name: LegCFICode
608.description: Multileg instrument's individual security’s CFICode. See CFICode (46) field for description

609.name: LegSecurityType
609.description: Multileg instrument's individual security’s SecurityType. See SecurityType (67) field for description

610.name: LegMaturityMonthYear
610.description: Multileg instrument's individual security’s MaturityMonthYear. See MaturityMonthYear (200) field for description

611.name: LegMaturityDate
611.description: Multileg instrument's individual security’s MaturityDate. See MaturityDate (54) field for description

612.name: LegStrikePrice
612.description: Multileg instrument's individual security’s StrikePrice. See StrikePrice (202) field for description

613.name: LegOptAttribute
613.description: Multileg instrument's individual security’s OptAttribute. See OptAttribute (206) field for description

614.name: LegContractMultiplier
614.description: Multileg instrument's individual security’s ContractMultiplier. See ContractMultiplier (23) field for description

615.name: LegCouponRate
615.description: Multileg instrument's individual security’s CouponRate. See CouponRate (223) field for description

616.name: LegSecurityExchange
616.description: Multileg instrument's individual security’s SecurityExchange. See SecurityExchange (207) field for description

617.name: LegIssuer
617.description: Multileg instrument's individual security’s Issuer. See Issuer (106) field for description

618.name: EncodedLegIssuerLen
618.description: Multileg instrument's individual security’s EncodedIssuerLen. See EncodedIssuerLen (348) field for description

619.name: EncodedLegIssuer
619.description: Multileg instrument's individual security’s EncodedIssuer. See EncodedIssuer (349) field for description

620.name: LegSecurityDesc
620.description: Multileg instrument's individual security’s SecurityDesc. See SecurityDesc (07) field for description

621.name: EncodedLegSecurityDescLen
621.description: Multileg instrument's individual security’s EncodedSecurityDescLen. See EncodedSecurityDescLen (350) field for description

622.name: EncodedLegSecurityDesc
622.description: Multileg instrument's individual security’s EncodedSecurityDesc. See EncodedSecurityDesc (35) field for description

623.name: LegRatioQty
623.description: The ratio of quantity for this individual leg relative to the entire multileg security.

624.name: LegSide
624.description: The side of this individual leg (multileg security). See Side (54) field for description and values

625.name: TradingSessionSubID
625.description: Optional market assigned sub identifier for a trading session. Usage is determined by market or counterparties. Used by US based futures markets to identify exchange specific execution time bracket codes as required by US market regulations.

626.name: AllocType
626.description: Describes the specific type or purpose of an Allocation message (i.e. "Buyside Calculated")

627.name: NoHops
627.description: Number of HopCompID entries in repeating group.

628.name: HopCompID
628.description: Assigned value used to identify the third party firm which delivered a specific message either from the firm which originated the message or from another third party (if multiple "hops" are performed). It is recommended that this value be the SenderCompID (49) of the third party. Applicable when messages are communicated/re-distributed via third parties which function as service bureaus or "hubs". Only applicable if OnBehalfOfCompID (5) is being used.

629.name: HopSendingTime
629.description: Time that HopCompID (628) sent the message. It is recommended that this value be the SendingTime (52) of the message sent by the third party. Applicable when messages are communicated/re-distributed via third parties which function as service bureaus or "hubs". Only applicable if OnBehalfOfCompID (5) is being used.

630.name: HopRefID
630.description: Reference identifier assigned by HopCompID (628) associated with the message sent. It is recommended that this value be the MsgSeqNum (34) of the message sent by the third party. Applicable when messages are communicated/re-distributed via third parties which function as service bureaus or "hubs". Only applicable if OnBehalfOfCompID (5) is being used.

635.name: ClearingFeeIndicator
635.description: Indicates type of fee being assessed of the customer for trade executions at an exchange. Applicable for futures markets only at this time. (Values source CBOT, CME, NYBOT, and NYMEX):

636.name: WorkingIndicator
636.description: Indicates if the order is currently being worked. Applicable only for OrdStatus = "New". For open outcry markets this indicates that the order is being worked in the crowd. For electronic markets it indicates that the order has transitioned from a contingent order to a market order.

637.name: LegLastPx
637.description: Execution price assigned to a leg of a multileg instrument. See LastPx (31) field for description and values

638.name: PriorityIndicator
638.description: Indicates if a Cancel/Replace has caused an order to lose book priority

640.name: Price2
640.description: Price of the future part of a F/X swap order. See Price (44) for description.

641.name: LastForwardPoints2
641.description: F/X forward points of the future part of a F/X swap order added to LastSpotRate (94). May be a negative value.

642.name: BidForwardPoints2
642.description: Bid F/X forward points of the future portion of a F/X swap quote added to spot rate. May be a negative value.

643.name: OfferForwardPoints2
643.description: Offer F/X forward points of the future portion of a F/X swap quote added to spot rate. May be a negative value.

644.name: RFQReqID
644.description: RFQ Request ID – used to identify an RFQ Request.

645.name: MktBidPx
645.description: Used to indicate the best bid in a market

646.name: MktOfferPx
646.description: Used to indicate the best offer in a market

647.name: MinBidSize
647.description: Used to indicate a minimum quantity for a bid. If this field is used the BidSize (134) field is interpreted as the maximum bid size

648.name: MinOfferSize
648.description: Used to indicate a minimum quantity for an offer. If this field is used the OfferSize (135) field is interpreted as the maximum offer size.

649.name: QuoteStatusReqID
649.description: Unique identifier for Quote Status Request.

650.name: LegalConfirm
650.description: Indicates that this message is to serve as the final and legal confirmation.

651.name: UnderlyingLastPx
651.description: The calculated or traded price for the underlying instrument that corresponds to a derivative. Used for transactions that include the cash instrument and the derivative.

652.name: UnderlyingLastQty
652.description: The calculated or traded quantity for the underlying instrument that corresponds to a derivative. Used for transactions that include the cash instrument and the derivative.

654.name: LegRefID
654.description: Unique indicator for a specific leg.

655.name: ContraLegRefID
655.description: Unique indicator for a specific leg for the ContraBroker (375).

656.name: SettlCurrBidFxRate
656.description: Foreign exchange rate used to compute the bid "SettlCurrAmt" (119) from Currency (15) to SettlCurrency (120)

657.name: SettlCurrOfferFxRate
657.description: Foreign exchange rate used to compute the offer "SettlCurrAmt" (119) from Currency (15) to SettlCurrency (120)

659.name: SideComplianceID
659.description: ID within repeating group of sides which is used to represent this transaction for compliance purposes (e.g. OATS reporting).

660.name: AcctIDSource
660.description: Used to identify the source of the Account (1) code. This is especially useful if the account is a new account that the Respondent may not have setup yet in their system.

661.name: AllocAcctIDSource
661.description: Used to identify the source of the AllocAccount (79) code. See AcctIDSource (660) for valid values.

662.name: BenchmarkPrice
662.description: Specifies the price of the benchmark.

663.name: BenchmarkPriceType
663.description: Identifies type of BenchmarkPrice (662). See PriceType (423) for valid values.

665.name: ConfirmStatus
665.description: Identifies the status of the Confirmation.

667.name: ContractSettlMonth
667.description: Specifies when the contract (i.e. MBS/TBA) will settle.

669.name: LastParPx
669.description: Last price expressed in percent-of-par. Conditionally required for Fixed Income trades when LastPx (3) is expressed in Yield, Spread, Discount or any other type. Usage: Execution Report and Allocation Report repeating executions block (from sellside).

670.name: NoLegAllocs
670.description: Number of Allocations for the leg

671.name: LegAllocAccount
671.description: Allocation Account for the leg See AllocAccount (79) for description and valid values.

672.name: LegIndividualAllocID
672.description: Reference for the individual allocation ticket See IndividualAllocID (467) for description and valid values.

673.name: LegAllocQty
673.description: Leg allocation quantity. See AllocQty (80) for description and valid values.

674.name: LegAllocAcctIDSource
674.description: The source of the LegAllocAccount (67) See AllocAcctIDSource (66) for description and valid values.

675.name: LegSettlCurrency
675.description: Identifies settlement currency for the Leg. See SettlCurrency (20) for description and valid values

676.name: LegBenchmarkCurveCurrency
676.description: LegBenchmarkPrice (679) currency See BenchmarkCurveCurrency (220) for description and valid values.
